PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER PERMITTING DISCOVERY OF THE FINANCIAL CONDITION OF DEFENDANTS DOROTEA GIANNINI AND THE ESTATE OF LUIGI GIANNINI; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; DECLARATION OF SARA SCORSONELLI, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION (TRANSACTION ID # 62519722) FILED BY PLAINTIFF SCORSONELLI, SARA HEARING SET FOR OCT-29-2018 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 302
MINI MINUTES FOR NOV-01-2018 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY, 302, PLAINTIFF SARA SCORSONELLI'S MOTION DISCOVERY OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND DECLARATION OF PLAINTIFF. MOTION DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION ALONE DOES NOT ESTABLISH THERE IS A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY THAT SHE WILL PREVAIL ON THE CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. RIVERA V. SASSOON (1995) 39 CAL. APP. 4TH 1045. MOREOVER, MANY OF THE STATEMENTS IN PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION ARE INADMISSIBLE HEARSAY AND PLAINTIFF HAS NOT INCLUDED IN HER MOTION ANY EVIDENCE THAT COLLABORATES THE ALLEGATIONS IN HER DECLARATION. THE RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO IT BEING REVISITED BY THE TRIAL JUDGE IF THERE IS A FINDING THAT DEFENDANTS ACTED WITH MALICE OR OPPRESSION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 AND SUBMIT THE ORDER TO JUDGE PRO TEM DAVID MCDONALD AT DAVIDMCDONALD@PRODIGY.NET. JUDGE: DAVID MCDONALD; CLERK: WILLIAM TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Off Calendar Nov-14-2018 Continued To Nov-15-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 504

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Assigned To Trial Court

Pl.S Motion Discovery Of Financial Condition And Declaration Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Nov-01-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SARA SCORSONELLI'S Motion Discovery Of Financial Condition And Declaration Of Plaintiff

Motion denied. The Court finds that Plaintiff's declaration alone does not establish there is a substantial probability that she will prevail on the claim for punitive damages. Rivera v. Sassoon (1995) 39 Cal. App. 4th 1045. Moreover, many of the statements in Plaintiff's declaration are inadmissible hearsay and Plaintiff has not included in her motion any evidence that collaborates the allegations in her declaration. The ruling is without prejudice to it being revisited by the trial judge if there is a finding that Defendants acted with malice or oppression. Prevailing party to prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312 and submit the order to Judge Pro Tem David McDonald at davidmcdonald@prodigy.net.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
